name: Dashboard Importer (backend)
description: Builds a Ragnarok workbook from a PyPSA dashboard model + GUI settings. Runs ENTIRELY in the Ragnarok backend (imports the bundled PyPSA source); the model is written straight into the session and never enters the browser. Backend port of the frontend dashboard importer — no separate server, nothing in plugins.env.

grp_build.label: Model & build
grp_build.description: Point to the model workbook and send the built model to Ragnarok.

send_to_ragnarok.label: Build & load into Ragnarok (server-side)
send_to_ragnarok.description: Builds the model from all settings below, entirely in the Ragnarok backend, and writes it into the session. The model never enters the browser. Does not run the solver — click the topbar Run when you're ready to optimise.

model_path.label: Model workbook path
model_path.description: Absolute path to the PyPSA model Excel workbook, resolved in the Ragnarok backend. Supports ${HOME} and ${PROJECT_ROOT}.

model_file.label: Model workbook
model_file.description: Upload a PyPSA model xlsx once — it is stored on the server (not in the browser) and picked from the dropdown. The build reads it server-side.

dashboard_path.label: Dashboard workbook (fallback)
dashboard_path.description: Optional path to a legacy dashboard.xlsx. GUI tables above always take precedence. Supports ${HOME} and ${PROJECT_ROOT}.

grp_time.label: Time & load
grp_time.description: Base/target year, the snapshot window, and the target annual demand.

base_year.label: Base year
base_year.description: Calendar year of the load/generation profile in the model workbook (e.g. 2024).

target_year.label: Target year
target_year.description: Simulation year — assets outside their lifetime range are dropped (e.g. 2030).

snapshot_start.label: Snapshot start
snapshot_start.description: Start of the simulation window in ISO format, YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM (e.g. 2030-01-01T00:00). The output snapshots are re-dated to this year — set the year to your target year so the timeline reads e.g. 2030, not the model's base year.

snapshot_length.label: Snapshot length (hours)
snapshot_length.description: Number of consecutive hourly snapshots. 8760 = full year.

target_load_twh.label: Target load (TWh)
target_load_twh.description: Rescale total annual demand to this TWh target. Set to 0 to leave unchanged.

grp_grid.label: Grid topology
grp_grid.description: How the network topology is treated, plus combined-cycle merging.

grid_mode.label: Grid mode
grid_mode.description: Network topology treatment applied before aggregation.

single_bus.label: Single bus name
single_bus.description: Bus name used when grid_mode is 'single'.

link_loss.label: Link loss fraction
link_loss.description: Fractional transmission loss applied to line-derived Links (e.g. 0.03 = 3%).

cc_rule.label: CC merge rule
cc_rule.description: Combine combined-cycle gas turbine blocks before aggregation. Edit rules in the right panel.

grp_agg.label: Region & carrier aggregation
grp_agg.description: Collapse buses into regions and/or merge generators per carrier.

aggregate_by_region.label: Aggregate by region
aggregate_by_region.description: Collapse buses into regions. Edit mapping and rules in the right panel.

region_column.label: Region column
region_column.description: Bus attribute used for region grouping.

aggregate_by_carrier.label: Aggregate by carrier
aggregate_by_carrier.description: Merge generators sharing the same (bus, carrier). Edit rules in the right panel.

grp_demand.label: Demand redistribution & adjustment
grp_demand.description: Move annual demand between buses/regions, or scale/add demand for a group (edit moves and rules in the right panel).

demand_redistribution.label: Redistribute demand
demand_redistribution.description: Move a fixed annual demand (MWh) between groups of buses/regions, proportionally over the existing profile. Runs after load scaling, before region aggregation. Edit moves in the right panel.

demand_adjustment.label: Adjust demand (scale / add)
demand_adjustment.description: Grow or shrink demand for selected buses/regions: multiply by a ratio, add a flat MW to every snapshot (split across buses by their demand share), or add an annual MWh (proportional scale). Runs after redistribution, before region aggregation. Edit rules in the right panel.

grp_replace.label: Generator replacement
grp_replace.description: Replace target-year plants with solar/wind (edit the table in the right panel).

replace_generators.label: Replace plants with renewables
replace_generators.description: Replace selected new power plants with solar and/or wind at the same bus, using the province's renewable profile. Pick the plants in the right-panel table — 'Fill table from carriers' adds every matching plant at once.

replace_build_year.label: Replacement base year
replace_build_year.description: Filter 2 (replacement only): only plants whose build_year ≥ this year are replaceable, and the Plant dropdown is filtered to them. Plants built before it (but still active in the target year) stay as-is. This is layered on top of the target-year filter — leave 0/blank for no extra restriction. Ignored when 'Include existing plants' is on.

replace_include_existing.label: Include existing plants (whole fleet)
replace_include_existing.description: Replace the entire fleet of the chosen carriers, not just new builds. When on, the 'Replacement base year' filter is ignored: every plant active in the target year is replaceable — so e.g. ticking this with coal selected replaces the whole coal fleet (existing units included). When off, only plants built on/after the base year are replaced. This also switches the 'Follow yearly additions' reference from each plant's build year to its CLOSE year (see that option).

replace_follow.label: Follow yearly solar/wind additions
replace_follow.description: Split each replaced plant's capacity between solar and wind by the ratio of solar:wind capacity ADDED in a reference year (across the model). By default the reference is each plant's build year; when 'Include existing plants' is on, it is each plant's CLOSE year instead (when it retires and the renewables come online), applied uniformly so plants of different vintages share one mix. When on, the fixed Solar %/Wind % boxes are ignored. If a year has no solar/wind additions, the split falls back to the latest earlier year with nonzero additions.

replace_max_close_year.label: Max close year (follow mode)
replace_max_close_year.description: Caps the close-year reference (used when both 'Follow yearly additions' and 'Include existing plants' are on): a plant closing on/after this year — or one that never closes — follows this year's solar/wind mix instead of its own (far-future) close year. Leave 0/blank to use the target year.

replace_carriers.label: Carriers
replace_carriers.description: Check the carrier(s) to work with. Filters the Plant dropdown to these carriers, and drives the 'Fill table from carriers' button below (which adds every matching plant, prefilled). None checked = all carriers.

replace_filter_column.label: Filter column (optional)
replace_filter_column.description: Optionally narrow the replaceable fleet further: pick any column from the model's generators sheet, then a value below. Applied together with the carrier filter (AND). Leave blank for carrier-only.

replace_filter_value.label: Filter value (optional)
replace_filter_value.description: Value the chosen 'Filter column' must equal — lists that column's unique values from the model. Only generators whose column equals this value (and match the carriers) are replaceable.

fill_reallocation.label: Fill table from carriers
fill_reallocation.description: Add every plant of the checked carriers that is replaceable (active in the target year and build_year ≥ the replacement base year, or the whole fleet when 'Include existing plants' is on) to the table below. Each added plant's Solar/Wind split is FROZEN at the current settings — change the settings and fill again to add another batch with a different rule; earlier rows are left untouched.

clear_reallocation.label: Clear table
clear_reallocation.description: Empty the Generator-replacements table — removes every row (filled or manually added). Nothing is replaced until you fill it again.

replace_solar_pct.label: Solar share (%)
replace_solar_pct.description: Direct percent of each replaced plant's original capacity assigned to solar. Used only when 'Follow yearly additions' is off.

replace_wind_pct.label: Wind share (%)
replace_wind_pct.description: Direct percent of each replaced plant's original capacity assigned to wind. Used only when 'Follow yearly additions' is off.

grp_ess.label: Energy storage (ESS)
grp_ess.description: Add a storage unit (ESS) at every bus where a generator was replaced above. Size it as a share of the replaced capacity or a fixed MW, and optionally make it capacity-expandable.

add_ess.label: Add ESS at replacement buses
add_ess.description: When on, one StorageUnit is built at each bus where the generator replacement above happened.

ess_carrier.label: ESS carrier
ess_carrier.description: Carrier name for the storage units. Added to the model's carriers automatically if it doesn't already exist.

ess_hours.label: Storage duration (hours)
ess_hours.description: Energy-to-power ratio (max_hours): energy capacity = power × hours. E.g. 4 = a 4-hour battery.

ess_efficiency.label: Round-trip efficiency
ess_efficiency.description: Round-trip efficiency (0–1). Split as √ into the charge and discharge efficiencies.

ess_sizing_mode.label: Sizing mode
ess_sizing_mode.description: How each ESS power rating is set: a proportion of the bus's replaced capacity, or a flat MW per bus.

ess_proportion_pct.label: Proportional size (% of replaced capacity)
ess_proportion_pct.description: Used when Sizing mode = proportional: ESS power = this % of the total generator capacity replaced at the bus (e.g. 30).

ess_fixed_mw.label: Fixed size (MW per bus)
ess_fixed_mw.description: Used when Sizing mode = fixed: every ESS gets this power rating (MW).

ess_capital_cost.label: Capital cost (overnight, per MW)
ess_capital_cost.description: Overnight (total) capital cost per MW of ESS power. The backend annuitises it over the lifetime below at the project discount rate. Drives capacity expansion; leave 0 for no cost.

ess_lifetime.label: Lifetime (years)
ess_lifetime.description: Economic lifetime used to annuitise the capital cost above (annuity factor at the project discount rate). Typical battery ≈ 10–15 years.

ess_expandable.label: Capacity expansion
ess_expandable.description: When on, the ESS power is extendable (p_nom_extendable = True) between the min and max below; the size above is the starting point.

ess_expansion_mode.label: Expansion bounds mode
ess_expansion_mode.description: How the min/max below are read: as MW, or as a % of the bus's total replaced capacity (e.g. 10% – 200%).

ess_p_nom_min.label: Expansion min (MW or %)
ess_p_nom_min.description: Lower bound on ESS power. MW in fixed mode; % of the bus's total replaced capacity in proportional mode (e.g. 10 = 10%).

ess_p_nom_max.label: Expansion max (MW or %)
ess_p_nom_max.description: Upper bound on ESS power. MW in fixed mode; % of the bus's total replaced capacity in proportional mode (e.g. 200 = 200%). 0 or blank → unbounded.

grp_mc.label: Marginal cost
grp_mc.description: Scale generator marginal cost per carrier (edit the table in the right panel).

marginal_cost_multiplier.label: Scale marginal cost by carrier
marginal_cost_multiplier.description: Multiply each carrier's generator marginal cost by a percentage of its ORIGINAL value (100 = unchanged, 30 = ×0.30, 130 = +30%). Edit the per-carrier table in the right panel.

grp_carbon.label: Carbon price
grp_carbon.description: Apply a carbon price to generator marginal costs.

carbonprice.label: Carbon price enabled
carbonprice.description: Apply a carbon price to generator marginal costs. Edit curves in the right panel.

carbonprice_scenario.label: Carbon price scenario
carbonprice_scenario.description: Scenario column name selected from the carbon price curves table.

currency_exchange.label: Currency exchange rate
currency_exchange.description: Model-currency units per one unit of the carbon-price currency (e.g. 1350 KRW per USD). Converts carbon-price-curve values into the currency your model's marginal costs use.

constraints.label: CF constraints enabled
constraints.description: Apply capacity-factor constraints to generators. Edit values in the right panel.

constraints_attribute.label: Constraint attributes
constraints_attribute.description: Comma-separated list of active CF constraint attribute names.

grp_output.label: Output & debug
grp_output.description: Optional map plot and debug workbook export.

plot_map.label: Plot map
plot_map.description: Compatibility flag from dashboard.xlsx (stored for parity, not rendered here).

export_path.label: Debug export path
export_path.description: Optional path to export the built workbook as XLSX. Leave blank to skip. Supports ${HOME} and ${PROJECT_ROOT}.

grp_tables.label: Reference tables
grp_tables.description: Tables backing the toggles on the left. Only the relevant ones are shown.

cc_rules.label: CC merge rules
cc_rules.description: How combined-cycle generator attributes are merged. 'others' row sets the default. Rule can be a reduction keyword OR a literal numeric value (1e5, 1e6, 1e7) applied verbatim to every merged generator. Active when CC merge rule is on.

province_mapping.label: Province → region mapping
province_mapping.description: Required columns: short, official. Optional group columns are looked up by the Region column selector. Active when Aggregate by region is on.

region_rules.label: Region aggregation rules
region_rules.description: Per-component attribute reduction rules. 'others' row sets the per-component default. 'default' / 'ignore' = skip (PyPSA uses 0 for p_nom). 'inf' / '-inf' = unbounded (stored as ±1e12). Or pick a literal value (1e5, 1e6, 1e7) to set every aggregated component to that number. Active when Aggregate by region is on.

demand_redist_moves.label: Demand moves (from → to)
demand_redist_moves.description: Each row is one move: it shifts 'Amount (MWh)' of annual demand from the source (From resolution + value) to the destination (To resolution + value). The two ends are independent and may use different resolutions — e.g. From bus '204' → To '전남' at group2. Each value dropdown follows the resolution chosen in that same row and shows that bus/region's annual demand (recalculated by the target load), so you can decide how much to move. Moves apply in table order. Region resolutions need a province_mapping.

demand_adjust_rules.label: Demand adjustments
demand_adjust_rules.description: Each row is one adjustment applied to the selected group (Resolution + Value, same choices as demand moves). Mode '× ratio' multiplies every demand cell (e.g. 1.1 = +10%). '+ MW (flat)' raises the group total by that MW at every snapshot, split across buses in proportion to each bus's share of the group's annual demand. '+ MWh (annual)' raises the group's annual energy by that amount via a proportional scale. Negative amounts decrease demand in the + modes. Rules apply in table order, after demand moves. Region resolutions need a province_mapping.

generator_replacements.label: Generator replacements
generator_replacements.description: Pick plants to replace with renewables. Rows added via 'Fill table from carriers' carry a FROZEN Solar/Wind split (the split under the settings at add-time), so different batches can use different rules and earlier rows stay put. Rows you add by hand compute live from the current settings: yearly solar/wind additions when 'Follow yearly additions' is on, otherwise direct Solar %/Wind % of original capacity. New units are added at the same bus/province with that province's solar/wind profile and a marginal cost = mean of existing same-carrier units. New units are named <plant>_solar_<year> / <plant>_wind_<year>. The dropdown lists the replaceable plants — active in the target year and built on/after the replacement base year, or the whole fleet when 'Include existing plants' is on (each option shows its build year). Active when Replace plants with renewables is on.

marginal_cost_multipliers.label: Marginal cost multipliers (% of original)
marginal_cost_multipliers.description: Per carrier: '% of original' is a percentage of that carrier's original marginal cost (100 = unchanged, 30 = ×0.30, 130 = ×1.30). Carriers with no row (or a blank %) are left unchanged.

carrier_rules.label: Carrier aggregation rules (static)
carrier_rules.description: Static attribute merge rules. 'others' row sets the default. Pick a reduction keyword OR a literal numeric value (1e5, 1e6, 1e7). Active when Aggregate by carrier is on.

carrier_rules_t.label: Carrier aggregation rules (time-series)
carrier_rules_t.description: Per-snapshot attribute reduction rules. Pick a reduction keyword OR a literal numeric value (1e5, 1e6, 1e7) to write into every snapshot of the merged time-series. Active when Aggregate by carrier is on.

constraints_rows.label: CF constraint values
constraints_rows.description: One row per (carrier, attribute, year). Filtered to target_year at build time. Active when CF constraints are on.

carbonprice_curves.label: Carbon price curves (per tCO2)
carbonprice_curves.description: Long format: (scenario, year, value). Value = price per tCO2 in the carbon-price currency; the Currency exchange rate setting converts it into the model currency. The active row matches the scenario name and target year. Active when Carbon price is on.

emission_intensity_rows.label: Emission intensities (kg CO2/MWh)
emission_intensity_rows.description: Per-carrier emission intensity by year. Filtered to target_year at build time. Active when Carbon price is on.
